...

- Launched HuggingFace transformer model for credit transfer evaluation by reverse engineering contractor pipelines.
...
amazon s3
...
multi-tenancy
...
Today I started at Dollar General as a Machine Learning Engineer. We're building a new team that will exploit the vast potential in DG's data to better serve our customers. We have big ambitions that will require a host of AI/ML techniques to realize. I'm looking forward to the new challenge!
...
WGU has been one of the best opportunities in my career. I accelerated through WGU's BS CS program in one term. Then I was hired at WGU in my first Python role as a Software Engineer. I'm grateful for the opportunity I had to internally transfer to Data Engineering as I worked through my MS CS. Around the time I graduated, I became WGU's first Machine Learning Engineer in our newly created Machine Learning team. In the years since, I've been privileged to contribute to high-impact AI/ML projects like AVA: https://lnkd.in/gXKRVeyE In the meantime, I've had the opportunity to work with a lot of great people. I've enjoyed the cross-team collaborations that made our successes possible. I will miss working with my team. And as a two-time alumnus, WGU's mission still resonates with me.
...
My team is hiring for a Senior Machine Learning Engineer. Feel free to DM for more information https://lnkd.in/gxt7Tnz4
...
I've been holding onto this one for a bit--our team's work on AVA (short for Academic Virtual Assistant), an AI powered on demand academic resource for students in their coursework, was recently featured on WGU's blog for its ability to support student outcomes with an ecosystem approach to AI technology.
...
AVA started very much as an exploration in "what if" and "how might we"? What if AI technologies could support positive learning behaviors, and how might we deliver an an experience to reach that goal? As much research has already highlighted the challenges associated LLM-based AI tools--erosion of knowledge and critical thinking skills, a focus on answers over learning--we wanted to see if we could offer something to students that avoided the harms and promoted the benefits of generative AI technologies. The result was AVA, a tool that lets students practice course concepts independently when instructors and other human supports are not available.
...
But AVA, in a positive twist, also promotes engagement with the broader learning ecosystem. We find that students who use AVA also reach out at higher rates to instructional faculty, and engage with their course learning resources at a higher rate as well, to the tune of nearly twice that of other students. It is still early innings for sure as we learn more about how to develop the experience for the best learning outcomes. A key learning is that AI tools can help students as point solutions, but likely offer more value as part of a broader instructional system made up of many support. Like many tech enabled tools before, experiences like AVA complement, not replace, the broader instructional ecosystem.

results.title: Dollar General shuffles executive ranks | Grocery Dive
highlights:
# Dollar General shuffles executive ranks
...
The discounter’s changes included naming Travis Nixon, who joined the company last fall to head its AI optimization efforts, as chief data and AI officer.
...
Dollar General announced several high-level executive moves on Thursday, including the appointment of new senior leaders for its information technology, artificial intelligence, store operations and real estate teams.
...
As part of the executive realignment, Travis Nixon, who joined Dollar General last November as senior vice president of artificial intelligence optimization, shifted to the role of SVP and chief data and artificial intelligence officer. In his new role, Nixon will oversee the discount retailer’s AI strategies and delivery operations, including data engineering and enablement. He will also continue to lead the company’s business process management initiatives, which involves using AI to improve efficiency in areas including merchandising, supply chain and store operations.
...
Dollar General also said Tom Hutchins moved to the role of SVP and chief technology officer. In his new position, Hutchins will expand his responsibility “across key technology areas to further strengthen enterprise alignment, performance and delivery,” the announcement noted.
...
The company also said Thursday that it promoted Josh Allison to division vice president of store operations, giving him oversight of over 2,400 Dollar General locations across the upper Midwest and northern Plains. The company also boosted Vai Anand to vice president of decision science and analytics, Michael Czubic to vice president of store operations, and Kevin Schoo to vice president of process improvement.

results.title: Dollar General AI Adoption Tracker | Larridin | AI Initiative Tracker
highlights:
Dollar General has appointed Travis Nixon as Senior Vice President of AI Optimization in November 2025, representing a strategic commitment to integrating artificial intelligence across merchandising, supply chain, and store operations[1][2]. The discount retailer has also partnered with AI companies like Shelf Engine to automate produce ordering across approximately 3,000 stores, utilizing machine learning to optimize in-stock levels and reduce food waste[3]. With nearly 21,000 stores serving predominantly rural markets, Dollar General is leveraging AI to enhance operational efficiency while supporting its retail media network and data collaboration efforts with CPG partners[4].
...
Senior VP of AI Optimization role with experienced hire from Dropbox/
...

Non-Tech Roles 6.1%
...
Dollar General shows limited AI hiring signals with only 4.5% of job postings mentioning AI. Notably, tech roles show 0% AI mentions while non-tech roles show 6.1%, suggesting AI expectations are emerging in business functions rather than core technology teams. Product roles lead at 28.6% AI mention rate.

### AI Optimization Leadership Role

Created Senior Vice President of AI Optimization position, hiring Travis Nixon to lead AI integration across business operations
...
Nixon brings over a decade of AI/ML experience from Dropbox, Meta, and Microsoft. Role focuses on using AI to identify chainwide optimization opportunities in merchandising, supply chain, and store operations
...
SVP of AI Optimization

### DG Media Network AI-Driven Personalization
...
Using artificial intelligence to improve campaign optimization, media planning and personalized offer delivery
...
AI enhances retail media capabilities across nearly 21,000 stores, focusing on localized and automated ad experiences. Supports data collaboration with CPG partners for targeted advertising
...
### AI-Powered Produce Ordering with Shelf Engine
...
Partnership with Shelf Engine to implement fully automated AI-powered ordering for produce inventory management
...
Deployed in approximately 3,000 stores by end of fiscal 2023. AI generates orders to optimize in-stock levels and provide customers with fresh produce. Follows successful two-phase pilot beginning December 2022
...
Dollar General is integrating AI across merchandising, supply chain, and store operations through a dedicated Senior VP of AI Optimization role. Key initiatives include automated produce ordering, retail media optimization, and data-driven personalization.
...
Dollar General partnered with Shelf Engine to deploy AI-powered automated ordering for produce across approximately 3,000 stores. The system uses machine learning to forecast demand and optimize in-stock levels while reducing food waste.
...
Travis Nixon was appointed Senior Vice President of AI Optimization in November 2025. He previously served as Head of AI for Dropbox's Security Division and held leadership roles at Meta and Microsoft.
...
Key partnerships include Shelf Engine for AI-powered produce ordering, Kevel for retail media technology, and Fetch for rewards-based advertising through their media network.
...
Approximately 3,000 stores use AI-powered produce ordering through Shelf Engine, while AI-driven personalization supports the retail media network across nearly 21,000 total stores.
...

Lead electronic communication model training by understanding, configuring and working with data scientists on NLP electronic communication AI models to find specific instances of secrecy, bribery, and other conduct issues at large financial institutions. Lead development of custom Conduct Surveillance key indicator lexicons to find customer specific conditions on which to alert and review Tune, optimize, and size Cloudera and Elasticsearch clusters per customer SLAs. Follow agile processes to manage development tasks including Sprint Planning and Grooming, Scrum, Retros, and Jira card management to meet major milestones as set by the PM and CSM.
...
💯 Everyone told Java developers that we would have to migrate to Python if we wanted to remain relevant in the AI world. 

They were wrong.
...
But in 2026, the tides have officially shifted. With the advancement of Spring AI, we're no longer "integrating" LLMs. We're building complex, type-safe, and scalable AI agents in the ecosystem we already trust.
...
I’m committing fully to Spring AI. Here’s why:
...
1. Type Safety > "Trust Me" Code: No more guessing if an LLM response will break your frontend. Mapping AI outputs directly to POJOs is a developer's dream.
2. The RAG Factor: Retrieval-Augmented Generation is the foundation of modern AI, and Spring’s VectorStore makes it feel like just another data source.
3. Enterprise-Grade: We don’t need "prototypes." We need security, observability, and dependency injection. Spring Boot gives us the framework; Spring AI provides the brain.
...
I’ll be sharing my journey, code snippets, and architectural patterns for building Production-Grade AI using Java.
...
Are you sticking with the Python stack for AI, or are you bringing AI into your Java microservices this year? Let’s discuss in the comments! #SpringAI #Java #GenerativeAI #SoftwareEngineering #SpringBoot #AI2026
...
Why Modern Java 25 + Spring AI is the High-Performance Engine for the AI Era.
...
If you still think Java is "just" about maintaining legacy enterprise monoliths, it's time to look again. With Java 25 (LTS) and the Spring AI framework, the platform has evolved into an engine designed for the massive scale that AI demands.
...
Here is why the Java 25 & Spring AI combo is the secret weapon for your AI strategy:
...
✅ Project Loom (Virtual Threads) – Scalability solved. You can now orchestrate thousands of concurrent Spring AI agents with simple, synchronous code. No more reactive complexity or blocked threads while waiting for LLM tokens.
...
✅ Records & Structured Outputs – This is how you handle AI data. Spring AI perfectly leverages Java Records. Mapping complex, unstructured JSON responses from an LLM directly into safe, immutable Java objects is now cleaner, faster, and 100% type-safe.
...
✅ Developer Velocity – With multi-line Text Blocks for your Spring AI Prompt Templates and a much more expressive syntax, Java finally "feels" as fast to write as Python, but with the raw power of the JVM.
...
By staying in the Java ecosystem, you aren’t choosing "old" over "new." You are choosing the most evolved, high-performance engine for the long haul. Java and Spring AI aren't just keeping up; they are setting a new standard for Enterprise AI.
...
Part 4 of my series on Spring AI. Bridging the gap between reliability and the future of intelligence.

results.title: Dollar General announces 9 officer appointments
highlights:
GOODLETTSVILLE, Tenn. – Dollar General Corp. today announced nine appointments to leadership positions across its real estate, information technology, business intelligence, human resources, and store operations departments.
...
• Tom Hutchins, transitioning to senior vice president, chief technology officer. Hutchins will be responsible for driving strategic planning and business integration initiatives while also expanding his oversight across key technology areas to further strengthen enterprise alignment, performance and delivery.
...
• Travis Nixon, transitioning to senior vice president, chief data and artificial intelligence (AI) officer, leading Dollar General’s overall AI strategies and delivery functions, including all data engineering and enablement functions.
...
DG announced Nixon as the company’s newly established AI officer in November. With today’s appointment, he will continue to lead enterprisewide business process management initiatives, applying AI to identify and drive optimization opportunities across the retail value chain in areas such as merchandising, supply chain and store operations.
...
Nixon arrived at DG with more than a decade of experience in machine learning and AI from several public technology companies.
...
• Vai Anand has been promoted to vice president, decision science and analytics, leading DG’s data science and analytics strategy.
...
Anand joining the company in 2021 as an experienced analytics and finance leader with deep expertise spanning data science, pricing, financial analytics and strategic analysis. Prior to joining Dollar General’s decision science team, he held leadership and analytics roles at retail, manufacturing, and financial services companies.

results.title: Dollar General appoints chief data and AI officer | Let's Data Science
highlights:
Retail Dive and Grocery Dive report that Dollar General announced nine officer appointments on June 15-16, 2026, covering information technology, artificial intelligence, store operations, real estate, and human resources. According to Retail Dive, Progressive Grocer, and Grocery Dive, Travis Nixon, who joined Dollar General in November 2025 as senior vice president of artificial intelligence optimization, was named senior vice president and chief data and artificial intelligence officer and will oversee AI strategy, delivery operations, data engineering, enablement, and enterprise business process management. Retail Dive and Progressive Grocer also report Tom Hutchins was named senior vice president and chief technology officer, and multiple promotions were made across store operations and decision-science teams. For practitioners, the elevation of a chief data and AI officer at a major value retailer underscores continued operational adoption of AI in merchandising, supply chain, and store workflows.
...
Retail Dive and Grocery Dive report that Dollar General announced nine officer appointments on June 15-16, 2026, spanning information technology, artificial intelligence, store operations, real estate, and human resources. According to Retail Dive, Progressive Grocer, and Grocery Dive, Travis Nixon, who joined the company in November 2025 as senior vice president of artificial intelligence optimization, was named senior vice president and chief data and artificial intelligence officer. Retail Dive and Progressive Grocer report the role consolidates oversight of the retailer's AI strategies and delivery operations, including data engineering, enablement, and enterprise business process management focused on merchandising, supply chain, and store operations. Progressive Grocer and Retail Dive also report Tom Hutchins moved to the role of senior vice president and chief technology officer, and the company announced promotions including Nick Snow to senior vice president of corporate store operations and Vai Anand to vice president of decision science and analytics.
...
Per Retail Dive and Grocery Dive, Nixon's responsibilities as described in the announcements include leading AI strategy, delivery functions, and continued leadership of business process management initiatives that apply AI to optimization opportunities across the retail value chain. Progressive Grocer notes Nixon's original hire in November 2025 created a dedicated AI optimization role, and Supermarket News includes an announcement quote from Steve Deckard regarding Nixon's appointment and the retailer's intent to integrate AI into operations.
...
The appointments at a large, low-cost national retailer are a practical indicator that AI efforts in retail are moving from experimentation toward operational scale, particularly in supply-chain, merchandising, and store execution workflows. Observers tracking enterprise ML adoption will view promotions in decision science and consolidated AI leadership as signals that model deployment, monitoring, and data enablement are becoming prioritized operational capabilities across retail field teams.
...
The appointment of a chief data and AI officer at a national value retailer is a notable signal that operational AI is advancing beyond pilots, affecting supply chain and store workflows relevant to practitioners. The story rates as notable rather than industry-shaking because it is an executive reorganization rather than a new technical breakthrough.

About Team The Catalog Data Science Team at Walmart Global Tech is focused on using the latest research in generative AI (GenAI), artificial intelligence (AI), machine learning (ML), statistics, deep learning, computer vision and optimization to implement solutions that ensure Walmart’s product catalog is accurate, complete, and optimized for customer experience. Our team tackles complex data science and ML engineering challenges related to product classification, attribute extraction, trust & safety, and catalog optimization, empowering next-generation retail use cases. We take pride in writing solid production code in Python, deploying and supporting model services and pipelines, and pushing the boundaries in latency, throughput, and scalability.

You will play a key role in supporting the attribute extraction platform and catalog data quality solutions — adding capabilities and driving adoption of GenAI-powered solutions to increase the coverage and accuracy of the Walmart's product catalog. The Data Science and ML Engineering community at Walmart Global Tech is active in most of the Hack events, utilizing the petabytes of data at our disposal, to build some of the coolest ideas. All the work we do at Walmart Global Tech will eventually benefit our operations & our associates, helping Customers Save Money to Live Better.

results.title: Distinguished Data Scientist: Associate AI Experience at Walmart | Jeremy
highlights:
Distinguished Data Scientist: Associate AI Experience at Walmart | Jeremy
...
# Distinguished Data Scientist: Associate AI Experience
...
Distinguished individual contributor role leading next-generation agentic AI systems and autonomous agents at enterprise scale across Walmart's global operations, combining deep ML/AI expertise with cross-functional mentorship and high-impact productionized solutions.
...
We are seeking a Distinguished Data Scientist to provide technical leadership and long‑term vision for next‑generation AI systems and platforms. This senior individual contributor will drive breakthrough system design and advanced solution development that transforms Walmart at scale. The role combines deep technical expertise (ML, simulation, optimization, agentic AI) with cross‑functional influence, mentoring of senior talent, and delivery of high‑impact, productionized solutions. You will be a great fit if: - You architect end-to-end ML/AI systems — from feature engineering through production deployment and monitoring - You’ve built and shipped multiple autonomous AI agents (not just experimented with LLM APIs) - You’re Level 5+ on Steve Yegge’s Vibe Coding scale and can ship production systems through AI-assisted development - You combine deep statistical rigor with practical business impact
...
About the Team The Associate AI Experiences team applies advanced data science, machine learning, and agentic AI to solve high-impact business problems across Walmart globally. You’ll build production Agentic systems, deploy autonomous AI agents, and translate complex data into actionable insights that drive measurable business value at massive scale. Our team builds intelligent platforms, agents and ML-powered solutions that make associates’ jobs easier, faster, and more fulfilling across stores, fulfillment centers, distribution centers, and home office. We leverage the Element LLM Gateway to safely access state-of-the-art language models while keeping all data within Walmart’s network. Our work spans NLP, computer vision, time series forecasting, and multi-agent AI systems at enterprise scale.
...
- Build and deploy production ML systems end-to-end: data pipelines, feature stores, model training, serving layers, monitoring, and feedback loops at scale
...
- Provide technical visionand lead advanced development in agentic AI, reinforcement learning, and simulation.
...
- Architect and deploy large-scale AI systems and autonomous AI agents — conversational assistants, predictive agents with tool-calling, and multi-agent orchestration systems using LangChain, Pydantic AI, and RAG patterns
...
- Deploy NLP pipelines at enterprise scale — semantic similarity across millions of records using BERT/SBERT embeddings and vector search (FAISS)
...
- Deep expertise in ML algorithms (XGBoost, CatBoost, LightGBM, Random Forest, AutoML) and deep learning (PyTorch, transformer architectures).
...
- Production experience with NLP (BERT, SBERT, FAISS, RAG), computer vision (YOLO, CLIP), and time series forecasting (ARIMA, Prophet).
...
- Hands-on experience building and orchestrating multiple AI agents with LangChain, RAG, tool integration, and memory management.
...
- Multi-cloud ML platform proficiency — AWS SageMaker AND GCP (Vertex AI, BigQuery, BigQuery ML).
...
- Strong Python skills (Pandas, NumPy, Scikit-learn) with containerization (Docker) and MLOps practices.
...
- Masters degree in Statistics, Economics, Mathematics, Computer Science, Information Technology, or related field AND 8+ years’ experience in data science or Machine Learning roles with demonstrated impact.
...
- PhD in AI, Machine Learning, Computer Science, Information Technology, Statistics, Applied Mathematics, Econometrics, or related field with relevant industry experience.

results.title: [Remote] Senior Generative AI Scientist II | Wfh
highlights:
# [Remote] Senior Generative AI Scientist II
...
Note: The job is a remote job and is open to candidates in USA. Cotiviti is a healthcare technology company focused on delivering better care at lower costs through advanced technology and data analytics. The Senior Generative AI Scientist II will work with a team to solve healthcare issues using machine learning and AI techniques, delivering value through innovative data solutions. Responsibilities • Work with key stakeholders within Research and Development as well as Business Operations, along with Product Management to assess the potential value and risks associated with business problems that have the potential to be solved using machine learning and Artificial Intelligence techniques • Develop an exploratory data analysis approach to verify the initial hypothesis associated with potential Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning use cases • Document your approach, thinking and results in standard approaches to allow other data scientists to collaborate with you on this work • Prepare your final trained model and develop a validation test set for QA • Work with ML Ops/Production operations to deploy your model into production and support them in monitoring model performance • Participate in design sessions to continuously develop and improve the Cotiviti machine learning platform • Participate in other data science initiatives, collaborating with your peers to support their projects • Participate in knowledge sharing sessions to bring new insights and technologies to the team • Provide End to End value-based solutions, including data pipeline, model creation and application for end user consumption • Complete all responsibilities as outlined in the annual performance review and/or goal setting • Complete all special projects and other duties as assigned • Must be able to perform duties with or without reasonable accommodation Skills • Graduate degree in a quantitative discipline such as Computer Science/Engineering, Statistics, Operations Research covering Advanced Statistics, Machine learning and AI • Experience with the latest techniques in natural language processing including transformers, fine-tuning LLMs, measuring/benchmarking and deploying LLMs with tools such as HuggingFace, Langchain, LLAMA/Mistral and OpenAI, vector databases • 5+ years of hands-on data science/AI experience, using typical machine learning and data science tools including pandas, scikit-learn, keras, nltk, and TensorFlow/PyTorch, GPU • Experience building production-grade machine learning deployments on AWS, Azure, or GCP • Experience working with Apache Spark™ and large-scale distributed datasets • Experience communicating technical concepts to non-technical and technical audiences is a plus • Flexibility to work with global teams as well geographically dispersed US based teams • Professional with ability to properly handle confidential information • Ability to work within a matrixed organization • Proficiency in all required skills and competencies above Benefits • Medical • Dental • Vision • Disability • Life insurance coverage • 401(k) savings plans • Paid family leave • 9 paid holidays per year • 17-27 days of Paid Time Off (PTO) per year, depending on specific level and length of service with Cotiviti. Company Overview • Cotiviti enables healthcare organizations to deliver better care at lower cost through advanced technology and data analytics that improve the quality and sustainability of healthcare in the United States. It was founded in 1979, and is headquartered in South Jordan, UT, US, with a workforce of 5001-10000 employees. Its website is Company H1B Sponsorship • Cotiviti has a track record of offering H1B sponsorships, with 165 in 2025, 118 in 2024, 90 in 2023, 102 in 2022, 72 in 2021, 67 in 2020. Please note that this does not guarantee sponsorship for this specific role.
